channel.title: The Soul of a New Machine — Audiobook
channel.description: Full audiobook of "The Soul of a New Machine" by Tracy Kidder, narrated by AI voices. A Pulitzer Prize-winning account of a computer engineering team racing to build a new machine at Data General in the late 1970s.
channel.itunes:author: Tracy Kidder

image.title: The Soul of a New Machine — Audiobook

channel.copyright: Original content © Tracy Kidder. This recording is a personal AI-narrated project.

item.title: Epilogue — A Good Life
item.itunes:title: Epilogue — A Good Life
item.description: The epilogue of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." A final reflection on Tom West, the engineers of the Eclipse Group, and what became of them after the Eagle took flight. A meditation on the meaning of work, craft, and the soul of engineering.
item.itunes:summary: The epilogue of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." A final reflection on Tom West, the engineers of the Eclipse Group, and what became of them after the Eagle took flight. A meditation on the meaning of work, craft, and the soul of engineering.

item.title: Chapter 16 — Dinosaurs
item.itunes:title: Chapter 16 — Dinosaurs
item.description: Chapter 16 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." Months after the machine's announcement, Jon Blau discovers a mysterious cable he's never heard of. The team reflects on what they built, the industry that's already moving past them, and the bittersweet reality that in computing, today's marvel is tomorrow's dinosaur.
item.itunes:summary: Chapter 16 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." Months after the machine's announcement, Jon Blau discovers a mysterious cable he's never heard of. The team reflects on what they built, the industry that's already moving past them, and the bittersweet reality that in computing, today's marvel is tomorrow's dinosaur.

item.title: Chapter 15 — Canards
item.itunes:title: Chapter 15 — Canards
item.description: Chapter 15 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." West's risky bet on PAL chips pays off, but the delay gives programmers time to create impressive software. The Eclipse Group refines the machine until boards can be swapped between Eagles without worry. The machine is ready for its public debut.
item.itunes:summary: Chapter 15 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." West's risky bet on PAL chips pays off, but the delay gives programmers time to create impressive software. The Eclipse Group refines the machine until boards can be swapped between Eagles without worry. The machine is ready for its public debut.

item.title: Chapter 14 — The Last Crunch
item.itunes:title: Chapter 14 — The Last Crunch
item.description: Chapter 14 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." In August, Carl Carman asks Ed Rasala when debugging will finish. The final push begins — the last crunch. The team works around the clock, chasing the last bugs, racing toward the finish line with the machine they've poured their lives into.
item.itunes:summary: Chapter 14 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." In August, Carl Carman asks Ed Rasala when debugging will finish. The final push begins — the last crunch. The team works around the clock, chasing the last bugs, racing toward the finish line with the machine they've poured their lives into.

item.title: Chapter 13 — Going to the Fair
item.itunes:title: Chapter 13 — Going to the Fair
item.description: Chapter 13 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." The Eclipse Group heads to the National Computer Conference in New York City — the industry's biggest fair. Alsing arranges a bus, and the team takes a rare day away from the basement to see what the rest of the computer world has been up to.
item.itunes:summary: Chapter 13 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." The Eclipse Group heads to the National Computer Conference in New York City — the industry's biggest fair. Alsing arranges a bus, and the team takes a rare day away from the basement to see what the rest of the computer world has been up to.

item.title: Chapter 12 — Pinball
item.itunes:title: Chapter 12 — Pinball
item.description: Chapter 12 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." The debugging continues through the summer heat. The lab is cramped and noisy, but the team pushes on. When they slow down to wait for parts, they notice the world outside — and the toll the project is taking on their lives.
item.itunes:summary: Chapter 12 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." The debugging continues through the summer heat. The lab is cramped and noisy, but the team pushes on. When they slow down to wait for parts, they notice the world outside — and the toll the project is taking on their lives.

item.title: Chapter 11 — Shorter Than a Season
item.itunes:title: Chapter 11 — Shorter Than a Season
item.description: Chapter 11 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." In the lab, the night shift bends low over wire-wrapped boards. The debugging grinds on, and the team's lives compress into the rhythm of the machine — a season of work that feels both endless and impossibly brief.
item.itunes:summary: Chapter 11 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." In the lab, the night shift bends low over wire-wrapped boards. The debugging grinds on, and the team's lives compress into the rhythm of the machine — a season of work that feels both endless and impossibly brief.

item.title: Chapter 10 — The Case of the Missing NAND Gate
item.itunes:title: Chapter 10 — The Case of the Missing NAND Gate
item.description: Chapter 10 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." Late in April, after the first deadline has come and gone, Ed Rasala examines his third revised debugging schedule. Dave Epstein walks in carrying a wire-wrapped board with three loose wires standing straight up — the very picture of a kludge. The debugging saga begins in earnest.
item.itunes:summary: Chapter 10 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." Late in April, after the first deadline has come and gone, Ed Rasala examines his third revised debugging schedule. Dave Epstein walks in carrying a wire-wrapped board with three loose wires standing straight up — the very picture of a kludge. The debugging saga begins in earnest.

item.title: Chapter 9 — A Workshop
item.itunes:title: Chapter 9 — A Workshop
item.description: Chapter 9 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." West's daily commute becomes a ritual of transformation — from the man who plays guitar at home to the hard-driving manager who runs the Eclipse Group. The basement of Building 14A/B is more than a workplace; it's a workshop where something new is being forged.
item.itunes:summary: Chapter 9 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." West's daily commute becomes a ritual of transformation — from the man who plays guitar at home to the hard-driving manager who runs the Eclipse Group. The basement of Building 14A/B is more than a workplace; it's a workshop where something new is being forged.

item.title: Chapter 8 — The Wonderful Micromachines
item.itunes:title: Chapter 8 — The Wonderful Micromachines
item.description: Chapter 8 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." The Microteam creates their own world within the basement — the Micropit, microawards, the microbus, and the outdoor microlounge. On Friday afternoons, while West meets with his managers, the Microkids go riding in the van, building their own culture within the culture of the Eclipse Group.
item.itunes:summary: Chapter 8 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." The Microteam creates their own world within the basement — the Micropit, microawards, the microbus, and the outdoor microlounge. On Friday afternoons, while West meets with his managers, the Microkids go riding in the van, building their own culture within the culture of the Eclipse Group.

item.title: Chapter 7 — La Machine
item.itunes:title: Chapter 7 — La Machine
item.description: Chapter 7 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." One morning shortly after the designing, West sits in his office staring at schematics. The machine begins to take physical form — boards, wires, chips — and the team confronts the reality of what they're building. The Eagle is no longer just an idea.
item.itunes:summary: Chapter 7 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." One morning shortly after the designing, West sits in his office staring at schematics. The machine begins to take physical form — boards, wires, chips — and the team confronts the reality of what they're building. The Eagle is no longer just an idea.

item.title: Chapter 6 — Flying Upside Down
item.itunes:title: Chapter 6 — Flying Upside Down
item.description: Chapter 6 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." West's rules of the game: compete for resources, promise the impossible schedule, sign up, and maybe get a reward. The brass tells the Microkids that Eagle is late and everything depends on them now. The pressure turns up, and the new hires discover what they've really signed up for.
item.itunes:summary: Chapter 6 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." West's rules of the game: compete for resources, promise the impossible schedule, sign up, and maybe get a reward. The brass tells the Microkids that Eagle is late and everything depends on them now. The pressure turns up, and the new hires discover what they've really signed up for.

item.title: Chapter 5 — Midnight Programmer
item.itunes:title: Chapter 5 — Midnight Programmer
item.description: Chapter 5 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." Carl Alsing introduces the author to "midnight programming" — the text adventure game that has swept through the basement of Westborough. Through the lens of Colossal Cave, we see the peculiar mindset of computer engineers: their love of puzzles, their patience with complexity, and the strange joy they find in getting lost in twisty little passages.
item.itunes:summary: Chapter 5 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." Carl Alsing introduces the author to "midnight programming" — the text adventure game that has swept through the basement of Westborough. Through the lens of Colossal Cave, we see the peculiar mindset of computer engineers: their love of puzzles, their patience with complexity, and the strange joy they find in getting lost in twisty little passages.

item.title: Chapter 4 — Wallach's Golden Moment
item.itunes:title: Chapter 4 — Wallach's Golden Moment
item.description: Chapter 4 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." Steve Wallach, a brilliant young engineer, has his moment in the spotlight as the Eagle project's architecture takes shape. The team grapples with the VAX's shadow, and the basement culture deepens with late-night debugging sessions and the peculiar rituals of computer engineering.
item.itunes:summary: Chapter 4 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." Steve Wallach, a brilliant young engineer, has his moment in the spotlight as the Eagle project's architecture takes shape. The team grapples with the VAX's shadow, and the basement culture deepens with late-night debugging sessions and the peculiar rituals of computer engineering.

item.title: Chapter 3 — Building a Team
item.itunes:title: Chapter 3 — Building a Team
item.description: Chapter 3 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." Tom West assembles his team of engineers in the basement of Westborough. We meet Carl Alsing, learn about West's pig roasts and his past at the Smithsonian, and see the culture of the Eclipse Group take shape — cubicles, late nights, and the peculiar language of computer engineers.
item.itunes:summary: Chapter 3 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." Tom West assembles his team of engineers in the basement of Westborough. We meet Carl Alsing, learn about West's pig roasts and his past at the Smithsonian, and see the culture of the Eclipse Group take shape — cubicles, late nights, and the peculiar language of computer engineers.

item.title: Prologue — A Good Man in a Storm
item.itunes:title: Prologue — A Good Man in a Storm
item.description: The prologue of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." Tom West, a mysterious computer engineer, reveals his character during a stormy sailing trip off the coast of Maine — a portrait of the man who will lead a team of engineers to build a new minicomputer at Data General.
item.itunes:summary: The prologue of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." Tom West, a mysterious computer engineer, reveals his character during a stormy sailing trip off the coast of Maine — a portrait of the man who will lead a team of engineers to build a new minicomputer at Data General.

item.title: Chapter 2 — The Wars
item.itunes:title: Chapter 2 — The Wars
item.description: Chapter 2 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." Tom West sneaks into a building to physically disassemble DEC's VAX computer, the Eagle project gets its name, and the internal "wars" at Data General over who would build the 32-bit machine are laid bare. A story of corporate espionage, engineering rivalry, and the race to build a supermini.
item.itunes:summary: Chapter 2 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." Tom West sneaks into a building to physically disassemble DEC's VAX computer, the Eagle project gets its name, and the internal "wars" at Data General over who would build the 32-bit machine are laid bare. A story of corporate espionage, engineering rivalry, and the race to build a supermini.

item.title: Chapter 1 — How to Make a Lot of Money
item.itunes:title: Chapter 1 — How to Make a Lot of Money
item.description: Chapter 1 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." The story begins at Data General, where Tom West and his team of engineers are racing to build a new 32-bit minicomputer — the Eagle — before the competition beats them to it. A deep dive into the culture, pressure, and personalities of the computer industry in the late 1970s.
item.itunes:summary: Chapter 1 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." The story begins at Data General, where Tom West and his team of engineers are racing to build a new 32-bit minicomputer — the Eagle — before the competition beats them to it. A deep dive into the culture, pressure, and personalities of the computer industry in the late 1970s.
